fullDisplayName: Shrine of Punishment SV90/SV94 Yellow A Alternate
rules:
Between turns, put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX (both yours and your opponent's).

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

fullDisplayName: Mt. Coronet SV89/SV94 Yellow A Alternate
rules:
Once during each player's turn, that player may put 2 Metal Energy cards from their discard pile into their hand.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

fullDisplayName: Brooklet Hill SV88/SV94 Yellow A Alternate
rules:
Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for a Basic Water Pokémon or Basic Fighting Pokémon and, put it onto their Bench, and shuffle their deck.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

fullDisplayName: Aether Paradise Conservation Area SV87/SV94 Yellow A Alternate
rules:
Basic Grass and Basic Lightning Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) take 30 less damage from the opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Search your deck for up to 4 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Look at the top 5 cards of either player's deck and choose 1 of them. That player shuffles the other cards back into their deck. Then, put the card you chose on top of that deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Put 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

fullDisplayName: Aether Foundation Employee SV81/SV94 Yellow A Alternate
rules:
Put 3 Pokémon that have "Alolan" in their names from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

fullDisplayName: Type: Null SV45/SV94 Yellow A Alternate

attacks.name: Armor Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The heavy control mask it wears suppresses its intrinsic capabilities. This Pokémon has some hidden special power.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Deep in the jungle, high in the lofty canopy, this Pokémon abides. On rare occasions, it shows up at the beach to match wits with Slowking.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They live in pitch black caves. Their enormous ears can emit ultrasonic waves of 200,000 hertz.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

attacks.name: Quick Draw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Current studies show it can evolve into an incredible eight different species of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Quick Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Royal Blades
attacks.effect: If you played Cynthia from your hand during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: The protuberances on its head serve as sensors. It can even detect distant prey.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Shiny objects are its passion. It can be found in its cave, scarcely moving, its gaze fixed on the jewels it's amassed or Carbink it has caught.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.
On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

attacks.name: Sparkling Wish
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Diamond Storm
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Fairy Pokémon.

flavorText: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.
The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

attacks.name: Divine Paper
attacks.effect: If your opponent has exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: One of the Ultra Beast life-forms, it was observed cutting down a gigantic steel tower with one stroke of its blade.

attacks.name: Moon Raker
attacks.effect: If the total of both players' remaining Prize cards is exactly 6, this attack can be used for Metal.

flavorText: One kind of Ultra Beast. Witnesses have seen it burn down a forest by expelling gas from its two arms.

attacks.name: Core Beam
attacks.effect: Discard a Metal Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When two Beldum link together, their psychic power is doubled. Their intelligence, however, remains unchanged.

attacks.name: Core Beam
attacks.effect: Discard a Metal Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its cells are all magnets. It uses magnetism to communicate with others of its kind.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin, while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: It has about three times the electrical power of Magnemite. For some reason, outbreaks of this Pokémon happen when lots of sunspots appear.

attacks.name: Searching Magnet
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Metal Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it's eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

attacks.name: Lord's Valley
attacks.effect: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, discard the top 10 cards of your deck.

flavorText: A dangerous Ultra Beast, it appears to be eating constantly, but for some reason its droppings have never been found.
It changes so it looks like its foe, tricks it, and then uses that opportunity to flee.

attacks.name: Sledgehammer
attacks.effect: If your opponent has exactly 4 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: A mysterious life-form called an Ultra Beast. Witnesses saw it pulverize a dump truck with a single punch.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon has lived with people since times long ago. It can sense when its Trainer is in the dumps and will stick close by its Trainer's side.

attacks.name: Missile Jab
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: They can detect the species of a living being—and its emotions—from over half a mile away. They control auras and hunt their prey in packs.

attacks.name: Detect
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It's tough enough to run right through the night, and it's also a hard worker, but it's still just a youngster.
To avoid attack, it mimics a tree. It will run off if splashed with water, which it hates.

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Knockout Reviver
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is Knocked Out, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.

flavorText: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.
It lures prey close with hypnotic motions, then wraps its tentacles around it before finishing it off with digestive fluids.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent's will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide.
It loves vengeful emotions and hangs in rows under the eaves of houses where vengeful people live.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: In battle, it uses its bladed tail to counter any Zangoose. It secretes a deadly venom in its tail.

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Cablegram
attacks.effect: If you have exactly 3 Prize cards remaining, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It appeared from the Ultra Wormhole. It raided a power plant, so people think it energizes itself with electricity.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.
Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute's time.
It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This carefree Pokémon has an easygoing nature. While swimming, it always bumps into boat hulls.
When the temperature cools in the evening, they emerge from water to seek food along the shore.

fullDisplayName: Alolan Vulpix SV8/SV94 Yellow A Alternate

attacks.name: Beacon
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It exhales air colder than -58 degrees Fahrenheit. Elderly people in Alola call this Pokémon by an older name—Keokeo.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it swings its burning tail, it elevates the air temperature to unbearably high levels.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

attacks.name: White Ray
attacks.effect: If you have only 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: One of the Ultra Beasts. It refuses to touch anything, perhaps because it senses some uncleanness in this world.
This Pokémon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake.

attacks.name: Sharp Blade Quill
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A bit of a dandy, it spends its free time preening its wings. Its preoccupation with any dirt on its plumage can leave it unable to battle.
This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

attacks.name: Twin Play
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Scyther and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: While young, they live together deep in the mountains, training themselves in how to fight with their scythes and move at high speeds.
